items.front: 8-1-6: When is action required for a missed oceanic position report?
items.back: When a position report affecting separation is not received, take action no later than 10 minutes after the control estimate unless otherwise specified.

items.front: Oceanic vs domestic missed position report deadlines
items.back: Oceanic (8-1-6): 10 minutes after the control estimate. Domestic (Chapter 5): 5 minutes for overdue aircraft.
1. Gross Navigation Errors (GNE). 2. Height errors. 3. Time (longitudinal) errors. 4. Intervention. 5. Special Area of Operations (SAO) verification. Under JO 7210.632.

items.front: 8-1-7: Governing order for oceanic error reporting
items.back: FAA Order JO 7210.632. The data collected supports risk modeling for separation standard reductions.

items.front: Why is oceanic error data collected under JO 7210.632?
items.back: For risk modeling activities to support separation standard reductions.

items.front: 8-1-8: Five inputs for calculating control estimates
items.back: 1. ATC automation system. 2. Known wind patterns. 3. Previous aircraft transit times. 4. Pilot progress reports. 5. Pilot estimates.

items.front: 8-1-8: How may control estimates be updated?
items.back: Through automated position reports and/or manual updates by the controller.

items.front: 8-1-8: When must control estimates be used?
8-1-9: Precondition for any non-RVSM aircraft in RVSM airspace
items.back: Must meet excepted aircraft criteria AND be previously approved by the operations supervisor/CIC.

1. Initial delivery to State of Registry/Operator. 2. Formerly RVSM-approved with equipment failure, flown to maintenance. 3. Mercy or humanitarian purposes. 4. Spare engine under wing within Oakland, Anchorage, and Arctic FIRs.
Exceptions are accommodated on a workload or traffic-permitting basis. All other 2-1-29 requirements remain applicable.

1. If no horizontal conflict: issue clearance to deviate. 2. If horizontal conflict but vertical separation possible: establish vertical then clear deviation. 3. If unable to establish required separation: advise unable, give traffic info, request intentions.
UNABLE (requested deviation), TRAFFIC IS (call sign, position, altitude, direction), SAY INTENTIONS.

items.front: 8-1-10: Pilot contingency procedures when communications lost after weather deviation
items.back: Follow AIP Section ENR 7.3 and AC 91-70, Oceanic and Remote Continental Airspace Operations.
Weather deviation requests should take priority over routine requests because aircraft will not fly into adverse meteorological conditions.
